metadata.summary: Meta Threads setup and content strategy. Text-first platform leveraging Instagram audience. Growth from conversational content, high cadence (7-10/day), reply velocity, not volume alone.

content.title: Threads Basics
content.summary: A setup and content strategy methodology for Meta Threads, a text-first social platform that leverages an existing Instagram audience. Growth is driven by conversational content (hot takes, questions, personal stories), high posting cadence (7-10 posts/day), and reply velocity — not just posting volume. The platform rewards authentic engagement; algorithm suppresses link-heavy posts and low-reply accounts.

why.p: Threads crossed 100M users rapidly, giving early adopters a significant reach advantage before competition densifies. Accounts that simply cross-post from X/Twitter underperform because Threads rewards shorter, more conversational formats. No official scheduling API exists as of 2026 — agents can draft content packs, but manual posting by the operator is required.

list.item: Standing up a brand or founder presence on Threads while leveraging an existing Instagram audience.
Drafting daily content packs (5-12 posts) for a SMM operator who has defined voice guidelines.
Auditing an account that cross-posts from X/Twitter verbatim and is underperforming.
Hot-take and conversation-starter ideation for accounts where engagement bait is acceptable.
Bootstrapping a new niche account that needs cadence and format scaffolding.
Target audience does not overlap with Instagram demographics (e.g., enterprise security buyers — use LinkedIn).
Brand voice forbids hot takes, personal stories, or unmoderated conversation.
Team has zero capacity for replies — Threads' algorithm rewards reply velocity; posting alone plateaus within weeks.
Regulated industries (finance, healthcare) where every post needs compliance review incompatible with the required cadence.

rule.statement: Use the same username as Instagram; keep bio under 160 characters with a clear what/who/CTA structure.
rule.rationale: Threads profile syncs from Instagram. Consistent username maximizes discoverability from the existing Instagram audience. Short bios with clear purpose outperform lengthy ones because Threads users scroll quickly.

list.item: Username: same as Instagram (auto-synced).
Display name: may include keyword for searchability.
Bio: 160 chars max, formula: [What you do] + [Who you help] + [CTA or personality].
Link: one URL; update based on current campaign goal.
Profile photo: same as Instagram (synced automatically).

rule.statement: Import followers from Instagram immediately after account creation to seed the initial audience.
rule.rationale: Threads provides a built-in follower migration from Instagram. Not importing leaves the account starting from zero when an audience already exists on a connected platform.

list.item: Create Threads account via Instagram.
Optimize profile (bio, link).
Import followers from Instagram.
Research successful accounts in niche.
Plan first week of content (minimum 5 posts/day).

rule.statement: Tag every draft with its content pillar; reject pillar-less drafts. Cap agent-generated posts at 50% of weekly volume.
rule.rationale: Without pillar tags, agents drift toward whatever style won last week. The other 50% must come from the operator's lived context (replies, real-time reactions) because the algorithm rewards authentic conversation, not publishing volume.

list.item: Opinions: industry takes, hot takes (2-3/day).
Value: tips, insights, learnings (1-2/day).
Questions: engagement drivers (1/day).
Personal: behind the scenes, stories (1/day).
Curated: interesting finds, quotes (1/day).

rule.statement: Post minimum 3 times/day; optimal 7-10 times/day. Reply within the first hour of each post for algorithm boost.
rule.rationale: Threads' algorithm rewards reply velocity, not just post volume. Accounts that post but do not reply plateau within weeks. Replies drive reach more than the post itself.

rule.statement: Keep agent-generated posts under 500 characters; validate length programmatically after generation.
rule.rationale: LLMs are unreliable at character-count limits. Over-length posts fail silently or look unprofessional on Threads' compact format. Include a hard length constraint in every prompt and check post-generation.

antipattern.description: Cross-posting X/Twitter threads verbatim to Threads.
antipattern.reason: Twitter irony, link references, and thread formatting do not translate. Threads rewards warmth and conversational register; verbatim X content reads as out-of-place and suppresses engagement. Force a register-shift rewrite step.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-posts to Threads on a schedule.
antipattern.reason: No stable scheduling API exists as of 2026. Meta is aggressive about automation detection. Best practice: agent drafts and ranks 20 candidates, operator picks 7-10 and posts manually within prime windows.

antipattern.description: Agent uses identical hook phrases across drafts ("Hot take:", "Honest question:").
antipattern.reason: Hook repetition reduces engagement. Enforce banned-bigram lists in the prompt to force variety across a content pack.

antipattern.description: Agent includes engagement-bait phrases: "comment X for Y", "tag a friend", "follow for more".
antipattern.reason: These phrases are flagged by Meta and suppress reach. Explicitly prohibit them in every prompt.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A Claude subagent can ideate the daily post pack from a content pillar mix, rewrite Twitter/X posts into native Threads voice, and draft reply scaffolds for the operator to send manually. The agent should NOT auto-post: there is no stable scheduling API, and Meta is aggressive about automation detection. Best division of labor — agent drafts and ranks 20 candidates, operator picks 7-10 and posts manually within the day's prime windows.

Best practices: Cap agent-generated posts at 50% of weekly volume; the other 50% comes from operator's lived context (replies, real-time reactions). Keep one human-written reply per outbound post — replies drive the algorithm more than the post itself. Tag every draft with its content pillar; without tags, the agent drifts toward whatever style won last week. Rotate prime windows weekly (8-10 / 12-14 / 18-21) and let the agent A/B test cadence within those slots. Strip URLs from agent output unless explicitly allowed; Threads suppresses link-heavy posts. Maintain a "do not post" list (tickers, regulated claims, named competitors) and inject it into every prompt as a hard constraint.

section.name: limitations-and-constraints
section.p: No official scheduling API at the start of 2026; tools that "schedule" Threads use unofficial automation that breaks regularly. Algorithm rewards conversation, not posting volume — agents that only post (do not reply) plateau within weeks. Cross-posting from X verbatim is a known anti-pattern; agents must rewrite, not duplicate, which costs tokens. Engagement metrics in native insights are limited; attribution to revenue is near-zero for organic Threads. Trend-jacking has a 24-48h half-life; agents running on slow cadence (daily) miss the window. Expect steady platform iteration and API changes.

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: Official API: Meta Threads API (Graph) with partial support (posting + insights for approved Meta apps; OAuth required, limited rollout). SaaS platforms: Buffer (native Threads scheduling via official integration), Hootsuite (native Threads support since 2024), Later (Threads scheduling, link-in-bio), Typefully (cross-posting X→Threads with rewrites), Metricool (Threads analytics + scheduling), Postiz (OSS scheduler with Threads support — operator hosts). CLI tools: instagrapi (Python, unofficial Instagram/Threads access — read only, breakage risk), threads-api (Node, community, read public posts; rate-limited and unstable), imagemagick/ffmpeg (prep optional 9:16 visuals), pandoc (convert markdown content packs into operator-friendly docs).

template.purpose: Founder, expert, and creator bio formulas.
Morning/afternoon/evening post templates and multi-post thread format.
Daily content pack ideation prompt with pillar tags and hook scoring.
